title: Open Data, Open Government
description: How open government data initiatives are enabling innovation, accountability, and better public services. Case studies from cities and countries leading the way.

title: Digital Ownership: NFTs Beyond the Hype
description: NFT speculation crashed, but digital ownership is more relevant than ever. From gaming items to digital tickets to creator royalties, practical applications are emerging.

title: XR Demo Showcase
description: Twenty XR startups demo their latest products. Try the newest headsets, haptic gloves, and spatial computing experiences in this hands-on exhibition.

title: Open Source AI: The Great Equalizer
description: How open-weight models from Meta, Mistral, and others are democratizing AI access. Panelists debate whether open source can keep pace with proprietary frontier models.

title: The Indie Game Survival Guide
description: Making a living as an indie game developer is harder than ever. Successful studios share strategies for discovery, community building, and sustainable development.

title: Board Games Meet Digital: Hybrid Play Experiences
description: The tabletop gaming renaissance meets digital technology. Designers show how apps, AR, and AI are enhancing physical board games without losing their soul.

title: Nuclear Fusion: Closer Than You Think
description: Multiple fusion companies claim they're within a decade of commercial power. Scientists evaluate the most promising approaches and the engineering hurdles remaining.

title: Managing Across Time Zones: The Async-First Playbook
description: Global teams spanning 12+ time zones share their communication protocols, decision-making frameworks, and tools that make async work actually work.

title: Casting in the Age of Social Media
description: Casting directors discuss how social media followings, self-tapes, and AI screening are changing how actors get discovered and cast.

title: Data Visualization as Storytelling
description: The best data visualizations tell stories that change minds. Information designers share techniques for making complex data compelling and accessible.

title: Climate Justice and Technology
description: The communities most affected by climate change often benefit least from climate tech. Advocates discuss how to ensure technology serves environmental justice.

title: Workplace Design for the Hybrid Era
description: Architects and workplace strategists share how physical offices are being redesigned for a world where people come in 2-3 days a week. It's not just adding Zoom rooms.

title: Tech Worker Activism: Voice, Exit, or Loyalty?
description: Tech workers increasingly face ethical dilemmas about their employer's impact. Activists and labor organizers discuss strategies for driving change from inside.

title: Governing AI: A Transatlantic Conversation
description: US and EU policymakers compare approaches to AI governance. Where they agree, where they diverge, and what it means for global innovation and safety.

title: Climate Storytelling That Moves People to Action
description: The data is clear but action lags. Communicators share narrative techniques that convert awareness into behavior change and political engagement.

title: Materials Science: The Invisible Revolution
description: New materials from graphene to metamaterials are enabling technologies that seemed impossible a decade ago. Materials scientists preview what's coming to market.

title: SXSW Wellness Morning: Yoga and Meditation
description: Start your day with a guided yoga and meditation session on the Austin Convention Center terrace. All levels welcome. Mats provided. Kombucha and smoothies after.

title: Creator Meetup: Afternoon Social
description: Casual networking for creators of all sizes. Share war stories, swap tips, and make connections that last beyond SXSW. Hosted at Cedar Door with drink specials.

title: Late Night Jam: Iron Works BBQ
description: An informal jam session at Iron Works BBQ featuring SXSW artists and surprise guests. Bring your instrument or just your ears. Free entry, cash bar.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: Platform Engineering: The Developer Experience Revolution
description: Internal developer platforms are becoming strategic investments. Leaders from companies with 1000+ engineers share their platform strategies.

title: The Creator Middle Class: Building Sustainable Businesses
description: Not every creator needs millions of followers. Hear from creators earning six figures with small, engaged audiences through courses, communities, and consulting.

title: Volunteer Tech Day: Code for Austin
description: Spend a day coding for local Austin nonprofits. Bring your laptop and skills — projects range from website builds to data analysis. Pizza lunch provided.

title: Natural Language to SQL: Democratizing Data
description: When anyone can query a database in plain English, what happens to data teams? Companies share their experiences deploying text-to-SQL tools across organizations.

title: Austin Running Club: SXSW Edition
description: A 5K fun run along Lady Bird Lake, followed by breakfast tacos and coffee. All paces welcome. Meet other active attendees and start your morning with endorphins.

title: The Physics of Game Worlds
description: Realistic physics makes game worlds feel alive. Engine developers demo next-generation physics simulation for destruction, fluid dynamics, and soft body mechanics.

title: Raising in 2026: What VCs Actually Want
description: A candid panel of VCs share their current investment theses, deal flow realities, and what separates a funding deck that gets a meeting from one that gets deleted.

title: Game Accessibility: Playing Without Barriers
description: How game developers are making their games playable by everyone, including gamers with visual, auditory, cognitive, and motor disabilities. Design patterns and testing strategies.

title: The Economics of Streaming
description: With subscriber growth slowing, streaming platforms are rethinking everything from ad models to content budgets. Industry analysts break down the numbers.

title: The Nonprofit Tech Stack in 2026
description: Nonprofits are chronically under-resourced in technology. A practical guide to the best free and affordable tools for fundraising, operations, and impact measurement.

title: Pickleball Tech: The Fastest Growing Sport Gets Smart
description: Pickleball is America's fastest growing sport and tech companies are paying attention. Smart paddles, AI coaching, and tournament platforms for the pickleball boom.

title: SXSW Music Showcase: Austin's Best
description: A curated showcase of Austin's most exciting emerging artists across genres. From psych rock to electronic cumbia, experience the city's legendary music scene.

title: AI Benchmarks Are Broken: What Should We Measure?
description: Standard AI benchmarks don't capture real-world performance. Researchers propose new evaluation frameworks that better predict how models perform in production.

title: The Claude Effect: How Anthropic Is Changing AI Development
description: Anthropic's head of product discusses the philosophy behind Claude, constitutional AI, and why safety and capability are not at odds. Includes a live demo of new developer tools.

title: Hiring in 2026: What's Changed and What Hasn't
description: Recruiting leaders discuss how AI screening, skills-based hiring, and portfolio assessments are replacing the resume-and-interview playbook.

title: Regenerative Agriculture at Scale
description: Can regenerative farming feed the world while healing the soil? Farmers, food companies, and scientists debate the promise and limitations of this growing movement.

title: Prototyping with AI: From Sketch to Code in Minutes
description: New AI tools can turn wireframes into working prototypes instantly. Live demos of the latest tools and honest assessment of what they get right and wrong.

title: Fandom in the Digital Age
description: How social media, fantasy sports, and creator platforms have transformed the fan experience. Fans are no longer passive consumers — they're participants and creators.

title: The Vision Pro Year One: Lessons Learned
description: Developers and users reflect on the first full year of Apple Vision Pro. What worked, what disappointed, and what's next for spatial computing.

title: Procedural Generation: Infinite Worlds Done Right
description: How to use procedural generation to create vast game worlds that still feel handcrafted. Technical approaches and design principles from studios that get it right.

title: Accessibility Is Not a Feature
description: Why accessibility must be a core design principle, not a checklist item. Disabled designers and engineers share tools, techniques, and cultural changes that make a real difference.

title: Mindfulness in the Age of AI Anxiety
description: As AI reshapes work and society, anxiety is rising. Meditation teachers and therapists share practical techniques for staying grounded amid constant change.

title: Misinformation in Election Year: What We've Learned
description: With 2026 midterms approaching, researchers and platform leaders discuss the evolving landscape of political misinformation and the interventions that work.

title: Music Rights in the AI Era
description: Who owns a song written by AI? What about a voice clone? Lawyers and musicians navigate the complex and rapidly evolving landscape of music rights.

title: Conversational AI: Beyond Simple Chatbots
description: Building AI assistants that maintain context, handle complex multi-turn conversations, and know when to ask clarifying questions. Design patterns from teams building production conversational AI.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: Burnout Prevention for High Performers
description: High achievers are particularly susceptible to burnout. Psychologists and recovered burnout sufferers share early warning signs and evidence-based prevention strategies.

title: AI NPCs: Characters That Remember and Evolve
description: Game developers showcase AI-powered non-player characters with persistent memory, emotional states, and dynamic dialogue. Live demos of the technology in action.

title: Data Privacy as a Human Right
description: Privacy isn't just a tech issue — it's a social justice issue. Advocates argue that vulnerable communities bear the greatest costs of surveillance capitalism.

title: Decentralized Social Media: Can It Work?
description: Mastodon, Farcaster, Bluesky — decentralized social media is having a moment. Protocol developers and community builders discuss what's working and what's missing.

title: MLOps: Getting Models to Production Without Losing Your Mind
description: The gap between a working notebook and a production ML system is enormous. Engineers share battle-tested MLOps practices for monitoring, versioning, and deploying models.

title: Remote Work Is Dead, Long Live Distributed Work
description: The return-to-office wars are over. What's emerged is a new model of distributed work that's neither fully remote nor fully in-person. Leaders share what's working.

title: The Middle Manager Renaissance
description: Once written off as obsolete, middle managers are essential to AI transformation. How to empower the people who translate strategy into execution.

title: Sports Betting and AI: The Perfect Storm
description: AI-powered sports betting is a billion-dollar industry. Oddsmakers, data scientists, and regulators discuss the intersection of technology, gambling, and sports integrity.

title: Design Tokens: The Bridge Between Design and Code
description: How design tokens create a single source of truth for colors, spacing, typography, and more. Workshop on implementing token systems that keep design and code in sync.

title: The AI Startup Playbook: Lessons from $10B in Funding
description: VCs and founders who've raised and deployed billions in AI startups share what separates the winners from the hype. Practical advice on moats, distribution, and timing.

title: AI Ethics Roundtable: Practitioners' Perspectives
description: An open discussion format where AI practitioners share the ethical dilemmas they face daily. No prepared talks — just honest conversation about hard choices.

title: Biotech for All: Democratizing Drug Development
description: Open-source biology, AI drug discovery, and decentralized clinical trials are lowering barriers to developing new treatments. Researchers share promising approaches.

title: The Data Lakehouse: Best of Both Worlds
description: Data lakehouses combine the flexibility of data lakes with the performance of data warehouses. Architects from companies running large lakehouses share lessons learned.

title: Esports in 2026: Beyond the Arena
description: Esports viewership rivals traditional sports. League operators, team owners, and broadcasters discuss what's next for competitive gaming.

title: SXSW Film Festival: Opening Night Screening
description: The highly anticipated opening night film of SXSW 2026. Details to be announced — but this slot has launched some of the decade's most talked-about films.

title: Energy Storage: The Missing Piece
description: Renewable energy is cheap but intermittent. Battery technology, hydrogen, and other storage solutions are racing to solve the grid's biggest challenge.

title: Electric Aviation: The Short-Haul Revolution
description: Electric planes are coming for regional routes. Aviation startups demo prototypes and share timelines for commercial electric flight under 500 miles.

title: Functional Mushrooms: Beyond the Hype
description: Lion's mane, reishi, chaga — functional mushrooms are everywhere. Scientists separate the evidence-based benefits from the marketing claims.

title: Game Audio: Composing for Interactive Worlds
description: Composers who score major games discuss the unique challenges of adaptive music, procedural audio, and creating soundscapes for open-world environments.

title: Enterprise Networking Breakfast
description: Start your day with fellow enterprise leaders. Continental breakfast, facilitated introductions, and roundtable discussions on this year's biggest challenges.

title: The Business of Touring in 2026
description: Tour economics have changed dramatically. Managers and agents discuss routing strategies, merch revenue, and how to make touring profitable at every level.

title: Sci-Fi Shorts: Visions of Tomorrow
description: A curated program of science fiction short films that explore AI, space, and humanity's future. Post-screening discussion with filmmakers.

title: Sustainable Fashion: Tech Solutions for a Wasteful Industry
description: Fashion produces 10% of global carbon emissions. Innovators share how AI-driven design, circular supply chains, and new materials are starting to bend the curve.

title: Digital Identity: Privacy, Security, and Access
description: Digital ID systems are expanding globally. Privacy advocates, government officials, and technologists debate how to build systems that are both useful and rights-respecting.

title: Carbon Accounting for Startups
description: A practical workshop on measuring and reducing your startup's carbon footprint. Covers frameworks, tools, and quick wins that don't require a sustainability team.

title: The Technical Co-Founder Shortage
description: Non-technical founders struggle to find CTOs. Alternative approaches including AI-assisted development, dev agencies, and fractional CTOs.

title: Building a Music Career Without a Label
description: Independent artists share how they built sustainable careers using direct-to-fan platforms, sync licensing, and strategic social media without traditional label support.

title: Green Hydrogen: The Fuel of the Future?
description: Hydrogen from renewable electricity could decarbonize heavy industry and shipping. But costs remain high. Scientists evaluate where green hydrogen makes sense.

title: Green Building: The Living Building Standard
description: Buildings that generate more energy than they consume, capture and treat all water, and use only non-toxic materials. Architects showcase projects meeting the strictest green standard.

title: AI Diagnostics: From Lab to Bedside
description: FDA-approved AI diagnostic tools are now in hospitals worldwide. Clinicians share real-world results and the challenges of integrating AI into clinical workflows.

title: Privacy Coins and Financial Surveillance
description: Privacy-preserving cryptocurrencies sit at the intersection of civil liberties and law enforcement. Cryptographers and regulators debate the future of financial privacy.

title: Cybersecurity for the AI Age
description: AI is both a tool for attackers and defenders. CISOs discuss the new threat landscape, including prompt injection, data poisoning, and AI-generated phishing at scale.

title: The AI-First Enterprise: Lessons from Early Adopters
description: CIOs from Fortune 500 companies share what worked, what failed, and what they'd do differently in their AI transformation journeys. Candid conversation, no vendor pitches.

title: CRISPR 2.0: Beyond Gene Editing
description: The next generation of CRISPR tools can edit RNA, regulate gene expression, and even detect viruses. Researchers preview applications from agriculture to medicine.

title: Fireside Chat: The Future of Human-AI Collaboration
description: A wide-ranging conversation about where AI is heading and what it means for creative professionals, knowledge workers, and society at large.

title: AI-Generated Art: Copyright, Ethics, and Creativity
description: Artists, lawyers, and AI researchers tackle the thorny questions around AI-generated images. Who owns the output? What constitutes fair use of training data?

title: Quantum Computing: What Can It Do Today?
description: Cutting through the quantum hype to show what quantum computers can and cannot do right now. Researchers demonstrate real quantum advantage on specific problems.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: The Creator Athlete: Building a Brand Beyond the Game
description: Athletes are building media empires. From podcasts to product lines, sports figures share how they've built businesses that outlast their playing careers.

title: Synthetic Data: Training AI Without Real Data
description: Why synthetic data generation is becoming essential for AI development, and how to avoid the pitfalls of model collapse. Hands-on examples with current tools.

title: The Carbon Removal Marketplace: Who's Buying and Why
description: With corporate net-zero deadlines approaching, the voluntary carbon market is booming. Buyers and sellers discuss what's working, what's greenwashing, and what needs regulation.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: Asteroid Mining: Science Fiction or Business Plan?
description: Companies are seriously planning to mine asteroids for precious metals and water. Geologists, engineers, and investors evaluate the technical and economic feasibility.

title: The Future of Retail in Spatial Computing
description: Virtual try-ons, spatial product browsing, and AR-enhanced stores. How spatial computing is changing the way we shop, both online and in physical stores.

title: The Live Music Economy in 2026
description: With ticket prices soaring and venues changing hands, the live music business is at an inflection point. Industry leaders discuss sustainability, access, and innovation.

title: Citizen Science: Everyone's a Researcher
description: Platforms that let anyone contribute to real scientific research. From galaxy classification to bird counting, citizen science is generating real discoveries.

title: Tokenized Real-World Assets
description: From real estate to art to carbon credits, tokenization is bringing traditional assets on-chain. Lawyers and builders discuss the legal and technical frameworks.

title: The 10-Minute Design Critique
description: A fast-paced session where audience members submit designs for live, constructive feedback from a panel of senior designers. Bring your work.

title: AR Navigation: Replacing the Map with the World
description: AR wayfinding inside airports, malls, and cities. How spatial anchoring, visual positioning, and 5G are making AR navigation practical.

title: Indie Film in the Streaming Era
description: With theatrical distribution shrinking, how do independent filmmakers reach audiences? Distributors, festival programmers, and filmmakers discuss the evolving landscape.

title: Designing Trust: UX for Sensitive Data
description: How fintech, health, and government apps build trust through design. Patterns for permission requests, data transparency, and error handling in high-stakes contexts.

title: The Science of Longevity: What Actually Works
description: Beyond the hype of biohacking and supplements, researchers share the evidence-based interventions that actually extend healthspan. Data-driven, no-nonsense approach.

title: Austin Food Crawl: Tech Meets Tacos
description: A guided walking tour of Austin's best food trucks and restaurants where tech founders moonlight as chefs. Networking with a side of breakfast tacos.

title: The Great Rebundling: Why Startups Are Building Suites
description: After a decade of unbundling, startups are building all-in-one platforms again. Founders and investors discuss why and when bundling beats best-of-breed.

title: Neuroscience of Creativity: What Brain Imaging Reveals
description: Using fMRI and EEG, neuroscientists show what happens in the brain during creative moments. Implications for education, therapy, and AI system design.

title: Technical Debt: The Silent Startup Killer
description: Engineering leaders share honest stories about technical debt that nearly sank their companies, and the strategies they used to dig out while keeping the product alive.

title: Social VR: Building Shared Virtual Spaces
description: The social platforms that survived the metaverse hype. Community builders share what makes virtual social spaces engaging enough to return to.

title: Virtual Production: LED Walls and Beyond
description: The Mandalorian popularized LED virtual production, but the technology has evolved dramatically. A hands-on look at the latest tools and techniques.

title: Computer Vision in the Real World
description: From self-driving cars to quality inspection in factories, computer vision has moved from research to production. Engineers share deployment lessons and failure modes.

title: AI Coaching: Can a Machine Make You Better at Your Job?
description: AI-powered coaching platforms claim to improve management skills, communication, and productivity. Users and researchers evaluate the evidence.

title: The Death of the Annual Review
description: Annual performance reviews are being replaced by continuous feedback, OKR check-ins, and AI-assisted performance insights. HR leaders share what's working.

title: International Co-Productions: Financing Global Stories
description: Cross-border film financing is opening doors for diverse stories. Producers share deal structures and creative strategies for international co-productions.

title: Animation in 2026: Tools and Techniques
description: From hand-drawn to AI-assisted, animation is more diverse than ever. Animators from studios large and small share their creative processes and favorite tools.

title: API-First Companies: Building for Developers
description: The API economy continues to grow. Companies built on developer-facing APIs share strategies for documentation, developer experience, and community building.

title: Building on Bitcoin: Layer 2 and Beyond
description: Bitcoin's ecosystem is expanding rapidly with Layer 2 solutions, ordinals, and new applications. Core developers and builders demo what's possible.

title: Fighting Algorithmic Bias: A Practitioner's Toolkit
description: Concrete tools and methodologies for detecting and mitigating bias in AI systems. Hands-on workshop with real datasets and open-source tools.

title: The Portfolio Career: Multiple Income Streams by Design
description: Why some professionals are deliberately building careers across multiple roles, companies, and projects. Financial planners and multi-hyphenates share how to make it work.

title: The Future of Food: Lab-Grown, Fermented, and Foraged
description: A taste-test panel featuring cultivated meat, precision-fermented dairy, and foraged ingredients. Can you tell the difference? Interactive tasting included.

title: Building AI Products for Non-Technical Users
description: The biggest AI opportunity isn't for developers — it's for everyone else. Product leaders share how they design AI experiences for people who've never written a prompt.

title: The James Webb Telescope: Two Years of Discovery
description: Astronomers share the most significant discoveries from JWST, from ancient galaxies to exoplanet atmospheres. Images and findings that are reshaping our understanding of the cosmos.

title: The GPU Shortage: Supply Chain Reality Check
description: With AI demand outstripping chip supply, how are companies securing compute? Infrastructure leaders discuss strategies from chip design to cloud optimization.

title: Ocean Tech: Innovations for Our Blue Planet
description: From autonomous underwater vehicles to kelp farming robots, technology is opening new frontiers in ocean conservation and sustainable blue economy.

title: Plant-Based Performance: Athletes on Whole Foods
description: Professional athletes who thrive on plant-based diets share their nutrition strategies, meal prep routines, and the science behind plant-powered performance.

title: Horror Shorts Showcase
description: A curated collection of the most unsettling, innovative short horror films submitted to SXSW 2026. Not for the faint of heart.

title: Brain-Computer Interfaces: The State of the Art
description: From Neuralink to academic labs, brain-computer interfaces are advancing rapidly. Neuroscientists discuss the technology, the ethics, and the timeline to consumer applications.

title: How to Get on SXSW Playlists
description: Music curators and playlist editors reveal what catches their attention and how emerging artists can get their music in front of the right ears.

title: Women in Tech: Breaking Through in 2026
description: Female founders and investors discuss the state of women in tech — the progress, the persistent gaps, and the strategies for building power and influence.

title: The State of AI: 2026 and Beyond
description: Amy Webb delivers her annual tech trends report, exploring how AI agents, multimodal models, and synthetic media are reshaping every industry. A must-attend keynote for anyone building or investing in technology.

title: Vertical AI: The Next Wave of Billion-Dollar Companies
description: Horizontal AI platforms had their moment. Now vertical AI companies focused on specific industries are attracting massive investment. Founders and VCs identify the biggest opportunities.

title: Building Developer Tools: A Love Story
description: Developer tools have some of the most passionate users and harshest critics. Founders of beloved dev tools share how they built products developers actually want to use.

title: Building Developer Platforms That Developers Love
description: Internal developer platforms can accelerate or frustrate engineering teams. Platform engineering leaders share what makes the difference between adoption and abandonment.

title: Cloud Gaming's Quiet Revolution
description: While VR grabbed headlines, cloud gaming steadily improved. An analysis of latency solutions, business models, and the games designed specifically for streaming.

title: The Exit Landscape: M&A and IPO in 2026
description: After a quiet period, tech exits are heating up again. Investment bankers and recently-exited founders discuss timing, preparation, and the current buyer landscape.

title: Remote Patient Monitoring at Scale
description: Hospitals deploying RPM to thousands of patients share operational lessons, clinical outcomes, and the technology stack that makes continuous monitoring feasible.

title: Education Technology in Underserved Communities
description: Technology can transform education — but only if it reaches the students who need it most. Educators and edtech builders discuss equitable deployment strategies.

title: The Future of Sports Broadcasting
description: From personalized camera angles to AI commentary, sports broadcasting is being reinvented. Network executives and tech companies preview what's coming.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: Breathwork and Nervous System Regulation
description: An experiential workshop teaching evidence-based breathing techniques for stress reduction, focus, and emotional regulation. No experience necessary.

title: Selling to Enterprise as a Startup
description: Your first enterprise deal can make or break your company. Founders who landed Fortune 500 customers share their sales playbooks, from first meeting to signed contract.

title: AI Regulation: What's Coming and How to Prepare
description: The EU AI Act is in effect, the US is drafting guidelines, and China has its own rules. A panel of policy experts breaks down what developers and companies need to know.

title: The 4-Day Work Week: Results from the World's Largest Trial
description: Researchers present findings from a global 4-day work week trial involving 10,000+ workers. Productivity, wellbeing, and retention data that challenges conventional wisdom.

title: The Obesity Drug Revolution and Its Ripple Effects
description: GLP-1 drugs have transformed weight loss. Endocrinologists, health economists, and patients discuss the medical, social, and economic implications.

title: International Startup Ecosystems: Beyond Silicon Valley
description: Founders from Lagos, Berlin, Bangalore, and Sao Paulo share what makes their local ecosystems unique and how they're competing globally.

title: Voice UX: Designing for Ears, Not Eyes
description: With voice interfaces in cars, smart speakers, and AR glasses, designers need new skills. Voice UX experts share principles for audio-first interaction design.

title: Gene Therapy: The Cures Arriving Now
description: After decades of promise, gene therapies are treating previously incurable conditions. Scientists and patients share stories from the frontier of genetic medicine.

title: Narrative Design: Telling Stories Through Play
description: Game writers and narrative designers discuss branching narratives, environmental storytelling, and how to make players feel like the protagonist of their own story.

title: The Death of the Hamburger Menu
description: Mobile navigation is being reinvented as gestures, voice, and AI predictions replace traditional menus. A provocative look at where mobile UX is heading.

title: Sleep Optimization: The Performance Multiplier
description: Sleep scientists and wearable tech companies discuss the latest research on sleep architecture, circadian rhythm optimization, and the tech that actually helps.

title: AI in the Military: Ethics and Strategy
description: As autonomous weapons and AI-driven intelligence become reality, military ethicists and strategists discuss the rules of engagement for artificial intelligence in warfare.

title: Creator Collaborations That Actually Work
description: Beyond basic collabs. How top creators structure partnerships, split revenue, and build lasting creative relationships that grow both audiences.

title: Writing for TV in the Post-Strike Landscape
description: How the writers' strike reshaped the television industry. Showrunners discuss new deal structures, AI clauses, and what's changed in writers' rooms.

title: Founder Happy Hour
description: The unofficial SXSW founder gathering. No agenda, no pitches — just cold drinks and good conversations with people who understand the startup grind.

title: Digital Inclusion: Connecting the Next Billion
description: Two billion people still lack internet access. Initiatives combining satellite connectivity, low-cost devices, and local content are closing the gap. Leaders share progress.

title: The Vinyl Revival: Analog in a Digital World
description: Vinyl sales continue to grow year over year. Record store owners, pressing plant operators, and collectors discuss what's driving this analog renaissance.

title: AI in the Newsroom: Automation Without Losing Trust
description: Major newsrooms are using AI for research, transcription, and even draft writing. Editors from the NYT, AP, and The Verge discuss where the line is between helpful and harmful.

title: Climate Data for Decision Makers
description: How satellite imagery, IoT sensors, and AI analytics are giving governments and businesses the data they need to make better climate decisions. Workshop includes hands-on data analysis.

title: The Patient Data Revolution
description: Patients are demanding access to and control over their health data. How interoperability standards and new regulations are empowering patients and disrupting healthcare IT.

title: Regulating Big Tech: What's Working and What Isn't
description: A global survey of tech regulation from antitrust to content moderation. Lawyers and regulators assess which approaches are having real impact.

title: Designing for AI: New Patterns for a New Paradigm
description: Chat interfaces are just the beginning. Designers from Google, Apple, and startups share emerging patterns for AI-native products that go beyond the chatbot.

title: XR for Education: Making Learning Immersive
description: Schools and universities using VR and AR to teach everything from anatomy to ancient history. Educators share measurable outcomes and implementation challenges.

title: Fine-Tuning vs. Prompting: When to Customize Your Model
description: A practical guide to deciding when prompt engineering is enough and when fine-tuning is worth the investment. Includes cost analysis, performance benchmarks, and case studies.

title: From Creator to CEO: Scaling Beyond Yourself
description: When your brand outgrows you. Creators who've built teams, launched product lines, and raised funding share the transition from individual to company.

title: Stablecoins: The Boring Crypto That Matters Most
description: Stablecoins process more volume than PayPal. A deep dive into the technology, regulation, and economic implications of dollar-pegged digital currencies.

title: DeFi for Normal People
description: Decentralized finance has the potential to democratize financial services. The challenge is making it usable. Teams building consumer-friendly DeFi products demo their work.

title: The Developer Experience of Web3
description: Web3 development is notorious for poor developer experience. Teams building better tools, documentation, and frameworks share progress toward making blockchain development accessible.

title: Psychedelics and Mental Health: The Clinical Evidence
description: With psilocybin therapy now legal in several states, researchers present the latest clinical trial data on psychedelics for depression, PTSD, and addiction.

title: Building Your Career Portfolio in the AI Age
description: The traditional career ladder is breaking down. Career strategists share frameworks for building a portfolio of skills, projects, and income streams that's resilient to disruption.

title: The Austin Taco Trail
description: A guided tour of Austin's most iconic taco spots, from food trucks to sit-down restaurants. Limited to 30 attendees for an intimate culinary experience.

title: Precision Medicine: One Size Doesn't Fit All
description: Genetic testing and AI are enabling treatments tailored to individual patients. Oncologists and geneticists share how precision medicine is transforming cancer care.

title: Building AI Agents That Actually Work
description: Moving beyond demos to production-ready AI agents. This panel explores the engineering challenges of reliability, tool use, and human-in-the-loop design that separate toy projects from real products.

title: Wearable Health: Beyond Step Counting
description: Continuous glucose monitors, blood pressure patches, and stress sensors. The next generation of wearables is turning consumers into patients and vice versa.

title: Digital Therapeutics: Prescribed Apps
description: The FDA has approved software as medicine. Companies building prescription digital therapeutics share the regulatory pathway, clinical evidence, and business models.

title: Designing for Aging Populations
description: By 2030, one in six people globally will be over 60. Designers share how to create products that serve older adults without being patronizing.

title: The Water Crisis: Tech Solutions for Scarcity
description: Water scarcity affects billions. Desalination breakthroughs, smart irrigation, and leak detection AI are making a dent. Engineers and policymakers share the latest.

title: AR in the Enterprise: Real ROI, Real Results
description: Enterprises are deploying AR for training, maintenance, and remote collaboration. Case studies with actual ROI numbers from manufacturing, healthcare, and logistics.

title: The CFO's Guide to AI ROI
description: How to measure the return on AI investments when the benefits are often intangible or long-term. Financial frameworks and case studies from early movers.

title: Building Inclusive AI Teams
description: Diverse teams build better AI. Hiring managers and DEI leaders share practical strategies for recruiting and retaining diverse talent in AI organizations.

title: The Producer's Toolkit: AI-Assisted Music Production
description: Music producers demo their AI-enhanced workflows for beat making, mixing, and mastering. Honest assessments of what helps, what hinders, and what's overhyped.

title: YouTube Strategy for Niche Creators
description: You don't need millions of subscribers to make a living on YouTube. Niche creators in education, crafts, and B2B share strategies for consistent revenue with focused audiences.

title: Sync Licensing in the Streaming Age
description: Getting your music in TV, film, and ads is one of the most reliable revenue streams for artists. Music supervisors reveal what they're looking for and how to get noticed.

title: SXSW Gaming Awards
description: The annual celebration of gaming's best — from indie darlings to AAA blockbusters. Live on stage with developer acceptance speeches and exclusive world premieres.

title: Motion Design for Product Interfaces
description: Subtle animations can make interfaces feel alive and intuitive. Motion designers share principles, tools, and examples of animation that serves users rather than distracting them.

title: The GLP-1 Generation: How Weight Loss Drugs Are Changing Food
description: With millions on appetite-suppressing medications, food companies are scrambling to adapt. Industry analysts discuss the ripple effects on restaurants, groceries, and agriculture.

title: The Mental Health Tech Landscape
description: From therapy apps to AI chatbots, mental health tech is booming. Clinicians evaluate what works, what's harmful, and what the research actually shows.

title: Podcasting Beyond the Mic: Distribution and Monetization
description: The podcast gold rush isn't over, but the playbook has changed. Learn about programmatic advertising, exclusive content models, and video-first podcast strategies.

title: Haptic Technology: Touch in Virtual Worlds
description: New haptic devices can simulate texture, resistance, and temperature. Engineers demo the latest hardware and discuss applications from gaming to surgical training.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: Mobile Gaming's Next Act
description: Mobile gaming revenue dwarfs console and PC combined. Where it's heading next, from Apple Vision integration to AI-personalized experiences.

title: The AI Hardware Race: Custom Silicon for Intelligence
description: From Nvidia's dominance to custom TPUs and neuromorphic chips, the hardware driving AI is evolving fast. Chip architects discuss what's next.

title: The Art of the Pivot
description: Some of the biggest companies started as something completely different. Founders share the moment they knew they needed to pivot, and how they executed the transition.

title: SXSW Design Awards Ceremony
description: Celebrating the year's best in interactive, product, and experience design. Finalists present their work before the winners are announced live on stage.

title: AI Tools for Creators: Amplify, Don't Replace
description: Creators share their favorite AI tools for video editing, thumbnail generation, copywriting, and audience analysis. Live demos of workflows that save 20+ hours per week.

title: From Zero to Revenue: Building a Business Model That Works
description: Forget growth at all costs. The era of efficient growth is here. Founders who bootstrapped to profitability share frameworks for building sustainable revenue from day one.

title: Tech for Good: Beyond the Buzzword
description: What does it actually mean to build technology for social impact? Nonprofits and social enterprises share frameworks for measuring real-world outcomes, not just engagement metrics.

title: Accessible Tech: Designing for Everyone
description: One billion people live with disabilities. Technologists and advocates showcase innovations in assistive technology, from AI-powered screen readers to neural interfaces.

title: Multimodal AI: When Models Can See, Hear, and Act
description: The convergence of vision, audio, and language models is unlocking entirely new product categories. This workshop walks through building a multimodal application from scratch.

title: The Emergence of AI Agents in Customer Service
description: AI agents are handling millions of customer interactions daily. Companies share metrics on resolution rates, customer satisfaction, and the art of graceful handoffs to humans.

title: Stadium Technology: The Connected Fan Experience
description: Modern stadiums are technology showcases. From 5G-powered replays to cashless concessions, venue operators share how tech is transforming the live sports experience.

title: DAOs That Work: Lessons in Decentralized Governance
description: Most DAOs failed, but some are thriving. Successful DAO operators share governance models, voting mechanisms, and treasury management strategies that actually work.

title: The Solo Founder's Guide to Shipping Fast
description: You don't need a co-founder to build a successful company. Solo founders share their playbooks for staying productive, making decisions, and avoiding burnout.

title: Women's Sports: The Business Case for Investment
description: Women's sports viewership and revenue are hitting records. Investors, league operators, and athletes discuss why now is the moment for women's sports business.

title: Retro Gaming Night
description: Classic arcade cabinets, retro consoles, and nostalgic multiplayer games. A social event celebrating gaming's rich history. Free play, drink tickets for attendees.

title: The Future of Clinical Trials
description: Decentralized, AI-designed, and adaptive clinical trials are accelerating drug development. Researchers and regulators discuss the evolving landscape.

title: Supply Chain Resilience in an Uncertain World
description: From pandemics to geopolitics, supply chains face constant disruption. How leading companies are using technology, diversification, and scenario planning to stay resilient.

title: Podcast to Screen Pipeline: Adapting Audio Stories
description: Podcasts have become Hollywood's favorite source material. Agents, producers, and podcast creators discuss the adaptation pipeline.

title: Open Source in Government
description: Governments are increasingly building and sharing open source software. Champions within federal and state agencies share wins, challenges, and how to contribute.

title: Sound Design Masterclass
description: Oscar-winning sound designers break down iconic movie moments and demonstrate how sound shapes emotion, tension, and story. Includes hands-on exercises.

title: Crypto's Comeback: What's Different This Time
description: After the crash and recovery cycle, crypto is rebuilding on stronger foundations. Builders share what they're working on and why this cycle focuses on real utility.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: The Freelance Economy Grows Up
description: Freelancing is no longer just a gig — it's a career choice for top talent. Platforms, benefits solutions, and community models that are making independent work sustainable.

title: SXSW Documentary Spotlight: Breaking Ground
description: A powerful documentary following three women in construction trades, challenging stereotypes and revealing the human stories behind America's infrastructure boom.

title: Festival Economics: Making Live Events Sustainable
description: Rising costs and environmental concerns are forcing festivals to innovate. Organizers share financial models and sustainability practices that keep festivals alive.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: The Ethics of Dark Patterns
description: Manipulative design is getting more sophisticated. Regulators, designers, and ethicists discuss where persuasion ends and manipulation begins.

title: Bootstrapping vs. Venture: An Honest Comparison
description: Two founders who took opposite paths — one bootstrapped, one VC-funded — compare notes on growth, control, lifestyle, and outcomes.

title: Electrify Everything: The Grid of 2030
description: Utilities, startups, and policy makers discuss the massive infrastructure buildout needed to support electric vehicles, heat pumps, and data centers simultaneously.

title: Short-Form Video Strategy in 2026
description: TikTok, Reels, Shorts — the algorithm is everything. Platform leads and top creators break down what's working now and what's changed in the last year.

title: Mars 2030: The Engineering Challenges Ahead
description: NASA and SpaceX engineers discuss the unsolved technical problems standing between humanity and Mars. From radiation shielding to food production, what needs to happen in 4 years.

title: The Research-to-Design Pipeline
description: How top teams translate user research insights into design decisions. Methods for synthesizing qualitative data and making research actionable, not just decorative.

title: The Future of Storytelling: AI as Creative Partner
description: Filmmakers who've used AI tools in production share their experiences. From script analysis to visual effects, how AI is changing the creative process without replacing artists.

title: Small Models, Big Impact: Running AI on the Edge
description: Not every AI task needs a 400B parameter model. Learn how quantized, distilled, and purpose-built small models are powering on-device AI from phones to industrial sensors.

title: Design Systems at Scale
description: How companies with 100+ designers maintain consistency without stifling creativity. Case studies from Spotify, Airbnb, and Figma on building and evolving design systems.

title: AI for Climate: Models That Matter
description: From weather prediction to materials science, AI is accelerating climate solutions. Researchers share breakthroughs in carbon capture optimization, grid management, and biodiversity monitoring.

title: The Metaverse, Revisited
description: The hype crashed, but the technology kept improving. A realistic assessment of where virtual worlds stand, who's using them, and what the actual path forward looks like.

title: Federated Learning: Privacy-Preserving AI at Scale
description: How organizations are training AI models across distributed datasets without sharing sensitive data. Technical deep dive into federated learning architectures.

title: SXSW Audience Award Screening: Midnight Selection
description: The midnight audience award selection — SXSW's wildest, most provocative film slot. Past selections have launched cult classics. Come expecting the unexpected.

title: The State of Play: Gaming's Biggest Year
description: A look at gaming's evolution as the industry surpasses $200B in annual revenue. Trends in mobile, cloud, and AI-generated content that are reshaping play.

title: Digital Twins: Mirroring the Physical World
description: Digital twin technology is transforming manufacturing, urban planning, and healthcare. Engineers show how virtual replicas of physical systems enable better decision-making.

title: The Documentary Renaissance
description: True crime, political, sports — documentaries are having a moment. Top doc filmmakers discuss the art and business of nonfiction storytelling in 2026.

title: The Microbiome Revolution: Gut Health and Beyond
description: New research linking gut bacteria to everything from mood to immunity. Scientists share the latest findings and what they mean for personalized medicine.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: SXSW Pitch Competition Finals
description: The top 10 startups from SXSW's pitch competition present to a panel of top-tier investors. Previous winners have gone on to raise $500M+ collectively.

title: Smart Cities: Privacy vs. Efficiency
description: Cities are deploying sensors, cameras, and AI to manage traffic, energy, and safety. Citizens and officials debate where the line falls between smart and surveillance.

title: Pricing Strategy for SaaS in 2026
description: Usage-based, seat-based, or outcome-based? SaaS pricing is more complex than ever. Revenue leaders share frameworks for finding the right model.

title: Voice AI: The Next Interface
description: With voice assistants getting dramatically better, voice-first products are becoming viable for complex tasks. Designers and engineers share what works and what doesn't.

title: AI Copilots at Work: Measuring Real Impact
description: Companies that deployed AI copilots to thousands of employees share hard data on productivity gains, quality changes, and unexpected second-order effects.

title: The Skills That AI Can't Replace (Yet)
description: As AI automates more cognitive tasks, which human skills become more valuable? Labor economists and hiring managers identify the capabilities worth investing in.

title: Telemedicine 2.0: AI-Enhanced Remote Care
description: Telemedicine has evolved from video calls to AI-augmented diagnostic platforms. A look at how remote care is becoming more capable than many in-person visits.

title: SXSW 5K Fun Run Results and Social
description: Post-race celebration with results, awards for top finishers, and a social brunch. Open to all attendees whether you ran or just want breakfast tacos.

title: Civic Tech That Actually Ships
description: Government technology projects are notorious for failure. Teams behind successful civic tech deployments share what they did differently, from procurement to production.

title: Building for Spatial: A Developer's Guide
description: Hands-on workshop for building spatial computing experiences. Covers visionOS, WebXR, and cross-platform frameworks for creating immersive applications.

title: The Psychology of Climate Action
description: Why knowing about climate change doesn't make people act. Behavioral scientists share evidence-based strategies for motivating individual and collective action.

title: Newsletter Renaissance: Owning Your Audience in 2026
description: With social algorithms becoming less reliable, newsletters are more valuable than ever. Top newsletter operators share growth, retention, and revenue strategies.

title: Health Equity and Technology
description: Technology can widen or narrow health disparities. Community health leaders discuss how to ensure new medical technologies reach everyone, not just the privileged.

title: The Psychology of Peak Performance
description: Sports psychologists who work with Olympic and professional athletes share mental training techniques applicable to any high-pressure field.

title: The Great AI Debate: Will AI Create or Destroy Jobs?
description: A structured debate between techno-optimists and labor economists on AI's impact on employment. Audience votes before and after. May the best argument win.

title: The Algorithm Athlete: AI in Sports Training
description: From computer vision analysis to personalized training programs, AI is transforming how athletes train and recover. Coaches and athletes share their tech stacks.

title: Music x AI: Threat or Tool?
description: Musicians, label executives, and AI researchers debate the impact of AI on music creation, distribution, and ownership. Featuring live performances with AI collaboration.

title: Building a Community That Pays the Bills
description: Paid communities are one of the most sustainable creator revenue models. Community builders share platforms, pricing strategies, and engagement techniques that retain paying members.

title: The CEO's AI Playbook
description: CEOs from mid-market companies share how they're personally engaging with AI — from learning to code prompts to restructuring their organizations around AI capabilities.

title: The Human Side of AI Transformation
description: Organizational change management for AI adoption. How companies are reskilling workers, redesigning workflows, and building AI-positive cultures without leaving people behind.

title: The Return of Enterprise Software
description: After a decade of consumer-style SaaS, complex enterprise needs are back in focus. Why the pendulum is swinging back to deep, configurable business software.

title: The Return of Skeuomorphism (Sort Of)
description: Flat design had its decade. Now spatial computing, haptic feedback, and nostalgia are bringing back rich, textured interfaces. What designers need to know.

title: Responsible AI in Healthcare Decisions
description: When AI recommends treatments or diagnoses conditions, the stakes couldn't be higher. Clinicians and engineers discuss building AI systems that are transparent, fair, and clinically validated.
venue: ACL Live at The Moody Theater

title: The Loneliness Epidemic and Technology
description: Social isolation is a public health crisis. Researchers and app builders discuss whether technology is causing loneliness, curing it, or both.

title: Prompt Engineering Is Dead, Long Live Prompt Engineering
description: With models getting smarter, is prompt engineering still a skill worth learning? Three practitioners share what actually works in 2026 and what's changed since the early GPT days.

title: Coding with AI: Pair Programming in 2026
description: How AI coding assistants have evolved from autocomplete to autonomous agents. Live coding demos show the current state of the art in AI-assisted development.

title: The New Space Economy
description: Space is no longer just government territory. From satellite internet to in-space manufacturing, commercial space is creating new industries. Investors and founders share the landscape.

title: Circular Economy Meetup
description: Connect with founders, investors, and operators building circular economy businesses. Short lightning talks followed by open networking. Snacks provided.

title: The Search for Life Beyond Earth
description: Astrobiologists discuss the most promising places to find extraterrestrial life, from Europa's ocean to Enceladus's geysers. What would discovering microbial life mean for humanity?

title: Election Security in the AI Age
description: Deepfakes, AI-generated robocalls, and synthetic social media accounts threaten election integrity. Security experts discuss defenses and early warning systems.

title: The Intersection of Gaming and Traditional Sports
description: Esports teams owned by traditional franchises, athletes streaming on Twitch, and games based on real sports. Where these worlds collide, interesting things happen.

title: Spatial Audio: The Sound of the Future
description: From Apple Music to live venues, spatial audio is changing how we experience music. Engineers and artists demonstrate the technology and its creative possibilities.

title: Music Tech Demo Day
description: Fifteen music technology startups each get five minutes to demo their products to an audience of artists, labels, and investors. Audience votes for their favorite.

title: Heat Pumps: The Technology Saving the Planet
description: Heat pumps are the most impactful climate technology most people don't know about. Engineers and policy makers discuss the massive rollout underway.

title: SXSW Music Showcase: International Night
description: A globe-spanning lineup featuring artists from six continents. From Afrobeats to Japanese city pop, experience the diversity of global music in one unforgettable evening.

title: Ethical AI in Practice
description: Moving beyond AI ethics principles to practical implementation. Teams share tools, processes, and organizational structures that make ethical AI development real.

title: Blockchain for Supply Chain Transparency
description: How blockchain is enabling verifiable supply chain tracking from raw materials to finished products. Case studies in food safety, luxury goods, and pharmaceuticals.

title: The UGC Revolution: Brands Want Your Content
description: User-generated content creation is a booming side hustle. UGC creators earning $10K+ monthly share how they land brand deals and create content that converts.

title: Retrieval Augmented Generation: Beyond the Basics
description: RAG is now table stakes. This advanced workshop covers hybrid search, re-ranking, chunk optimization, and evaluation frameworks for production RAG systems.
